title: Sempra (SRE) Economic Prospect Score — Consensus Analysis
description: Consensus economic prospect analysis of Sempra (SRE), averaging independent scores from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1 across competitive momentum, moat durability, and sentiment catalysts.
verdictDetail: Sempra benefits from regulated utility cash flows in California and Texas, providing a stable foundation. However, flat revenue growth and negative free cash flow reflect heavy ongoing infrastructure investments, limiting near-term upside.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: Stable but slow growth inherent to utilities.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: Recent revenue growth of -0.2% is sluggish, reflecting the nature of regulated utility returns and varying energy prices. Growth is primarily driven by approved rate base expansions rather than organic volume increases.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: Sempra holds regional monopolies in Southern California (SDG&E, SoCalGas) and a strong presence in Texas (Oncor). Market share is highly protected by geographic boundaries and regulatory mandates.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: Pricing power is strictly regulated by public utility commissions. While they can request rate hikes to cover investments, these are subject to political and regulatory approval, not market forces.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: Infrastructure projects like LNG export terminals and grid modernization take years to develop and execute. Product velocity is inherently low in the utility sector.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: Monopolistic advantages and massive infrastructure investments create a wide moat.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: Consumers and businesses have effectively zero ability to switch electricity or gas distribution providers in Sempra's service territories, creating near-absolute customer lock-in.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: While true network effects are minimal, the sheer density of their distribution grid creates localized economies of scale that are impossible for a newcomer to replicate.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: Sempra's primary moat is regulatory. State-granted monopolies ensure they are the sole provider of critical energy infrastructure in their regions, protecting them from direct competition.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: The tens of billions of dollars required to build and maintain power grids and gas pipelines serve as an insurmountable barrier to entry for potential challengers.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Utility sentiment relies on interest rates and regulatory environments.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Earnings are generally predictable due to regulated returns. Revisions are infrequent and typically driven by changes in allowable return on equity or major project approvals.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: News sentiment is mixed, balancing the appeal of LNG export growth against public backlash over rising utility rates in California.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: Management has successfully focused the company on high-growth areas like Texas T&D and LNG infrastructure. However, the massive capital required for these projects has led to deeply negative free cash flow.

keyRisks: Adverse regulatory decisions in California or Texas restricting allowable returns on equity.
Delays or cost overruns on massive infrastructure projects like LNG export terminals.
Increased risk of wildfires in California exposing the company to significant liability.
keyCatalysts: Successful completion and commissioning of major LNG export facilities.
Favorable regulatory approvals for rate base expansions in Texas and California.
A decline in interest rates making dividend-paying utilities more attractive to income investors.
methodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored SRE at 65/100 and Opus at 65/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
